# Test: Gitea Unreachable Error Handling (Edge Case)
# Validates graceful error handling when Gitea is not accessible.
#
# Prerequisites: k3d and Gitea running (handled by TestE2E)
# Environment: GITEA_URL, GITEA_TOKEN, E2E_CLUSTER are injected by test runner

# Step 1: Create app repository
mkdir app
cd app
exec git init
exec git config user.name 'E2E Test Bot'
exec git config user.email 'e2e@example.com'
exec apx init app --org=testorg --repo=test-app --non-interactive internal/apis/proto/unreachable/v1
exists apx.yaml

exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'Initial schema'
exec git tag proto/unreachable/v1/v1.0.0

# Step 2: Try to publish with an invalid Gitea URL
# This should fail with a connection error, not a panic
! exec apx publish --module-path=internal/apis/proto/unreachable/v1 --canonical-repo=http://localhost:9999/testorg/nonexistent
stderr '.'

# Step 3: Verify the app repo is still intact after failed publish
exists apx.yaml
exists internal/apis/proto/unreachable/v1/unreachable.proto
exec git log --oneline
stdout 'Initial schema'

cd ..

# Success: Gitea unreachable error handling validated
# - Publish fails gracefully when Gitea is unreachable
# - Local repository state is not corrupted
